purpose: Pin the contract any future evidence-ring stress harness must satisfy. Under high-core agent-swarm workloads the runtime evidence rings, stdio evidence rows, and membrane validation logs WILL exceed their bounded capacity in both strict and hardened modes; this contract requires that the resulting loss is intentional, bounded, monotone-counted, and deterministically serialized so downstream replay can distinguish a missing row from corruption. NO production ring geometry changes are required by this bead — the manifest gates the *shape* of the report any future stress run must produce.

ring_paths.downstream_replay_treatment: Replay must treat any seqno gap below `(max_epoch * capacity)` as expected loss attributed to backpressure, not corruption. The minimizer (bd-juvqm.6) emits an empty signature when the loss is fully accounted for by `evidence_loss_count`; otherwise it emits `evidence_seqno_gap_exceeded_loss_count` and fails replay closed.
Same as decision_card_ring: gaps below loss_count are expected; gaps above fail closed.
Replay tools must read the loss_count and max_epoch fields and treat gaps below the threshold as expected. Otherwise the structured log is unrecoverable for that interval.
The stdio evidence ring records FILE-stream observations; downstream replay treats missing rows as evidence loss attributed to the FILE-membrane lock geometry under high-core load.

Membrane validation logs may overwrite during heavy-shard contention; downstream replay may interpret a gap as evidence that the canary or bounds path was exercised more than `capacity` times in the interval.

stress_profile.rationale: Drive each ring to 4x capacity. That guarantees at least 3x capacity worth of overwrites — enough to verify that overwrite_oldest is correct, that evidence_loss_count is monotone-incrementing, and that max_epoch advances at least 3 times.

real_ring_report.rationale: When the synthetic ModelRing contract is exercised against the production `frankenlibc_membrane::runtime_math::evidence::EvidenceRingBuffer<CAP>`, the runner emits a structured `RealRingReport` JSONL row. The production ring overwrites without explicitly counting losses — the contract reconstructs `derived_loss = total_pushed - snapshot_size` and validates monotone seqno against the snapshot.

loss_evidence_kinds.rationale: Loss is fully accounted for by evidence_loss_count and the seqno gap matches. Replay treats this as intentional backpressure.
Stress run produced gaps but did not export evidence_loss_count. Must fail closed because replay cannot distinguish from corruption.
evidence_loss_count is smaller than the observed seqno gap. Indicates a counting bug or a non-overwrite-oldest loss path.
A reader observed a smaller seqno after a larger one within the same epoch. Always indicates a publication bug.
Two stress runs with the same seed produced byte-different snapshots after overwrite. Replay correctness depends on this being deterministic.

downstream_consumers.consumes: The decision-trace minimizer treats `evidence_loss_count` and the documented loss_evidence_kinds as inputs when classifying gap signatures.
The membrane contention attribution gate's `evidence_emission` and `stdio_sync` stages read the per-ring loss accounting recorded here.
The user-facing explain dossier surfaces loss_count + max_epoch so a user can see how much evidence was overwritten between two replays.

summary.next_safe_action: When a future stress harness runs against the real evidence ring, it MUST emit per-ring rows containing every required_ring_path_field and only loss_evidence_kinds documented here. Adding a new loss kind requires updating this manifest atomically with source_commit.
